description: O*NET Knowledge domains (2.C) with behavioral anchors

2.C.name: Knowledge
2.C.description: Organized sets of principles and facts applying in general domains

2.C.1.name: Business and Management
2.C.1.description: Knowledge of principles and facts related to business administration and accounting, human and material resource management in organizations, sales and marketing, economics, and office information and organizing systems

2.C.1.a.name: Administration and Management
2.C.1.a.description: Knowledge of business and management principles involved in strategic planning, resource allocation, human resources modeling, leadership technique, production methods, and coordination of people and resources.

2.C.1.b.name: Administrative
2.C.1.b.description: Knowledge of administrative and office procedures and systems such as word processing, managing files and records, stenography and transcription, designing forms, and workplace terminology.

2.C.1.c.name: Economics and Accounting
2.C.1.c.description: Knowledge of economic and accounting principles and practices, the financial markets, banking, and the analysis and reporting of financial data.

2.C.1.d.name: Sales and Marketing
2.C.1.d.description: Knowledge of principles and methods for showing, promoting, and selling products or services. This includes marketing strategy and tactics, product demonstration, sales techniques, and sales control systems.

2.C.1.e.name: Customer and Personal Service
2.C.1.e.description: Knowledge of principles and processes for providing customer and personal services. This includes customer needs assessment, meeting quality standards for services, and evaluation of customer satisfaction.

2.C.1.f.name: Personnel and Human Resources
2.C.1.f.description: Knowledge of principles and procedures for personnel recruitment, selection, training, compensation and benefits, labor relations and negotiation, and personnel information systems.

2.C.2.name: Manufacturing and Production
2.C.2.description: Knowledge of principles and facts related to the production, processing, storage, and distribution of manufactured and agricultural goods

2.C.2.a.name: Production and Processing
2.C.2.a.description: Knowledge of raw materials, production processes, quality control, costs, and other techniques for maximizing the effective manufacture and distribution of goods.

2.C.2.b.name: Food Production
2.C.2.b.description: Knowledge of techniques and equipment for planting, growing, and harvesting food products (both plant and animal) for consumption, including storage/handling techniques.

2.C.3.name: Engineering and Technology
2.C.3.description: Knowledge of the design, development, and application of technology for specific purposes.

2.C.3.a.name: Computers and Electronics
2.C.3.a.description: Knowledge of circuit boards, processors, chips, electronic equipment, and computer hardware and software, including applications and programming.

2.C.3.b.name: Engineering and Technology
2.C.3.b.description: Knowledge of the practical application of engineering science and technology. This includes applying principles, techniques, procedures, and equipment to the design and production of various goods and services.

2.C.3.c.name: Design
2.C.3.c.description: Knowledge of design techniques, tools, and principles involved in production of precision technical plans, blueprints, drawings, and models.

2.C.3.d.name: Building and Construction
2.C.3.d.description: Knowledge of materials, methods, and the tools involved in the construction or repair of houses, buildings, or other structures such as highways and roads.

2.C.3.e.name: Mechanical
2.C.3.e.description: Knowledge of machines and tools, including their designs, uses, repair, and maintenance.

2.C.4.name: Mathematics and Science
2.C.4.description: Knowledge of the history, theories, methods, and applications of the physical, biological, social, mathematical, and geography

2.C.4.a.name: Mathematics
2.C.4.a.description: Knowledge of arithmetic, algebra, geometry, calculus, statistics, and their applications.

2.C.4.b.name: Physics
2.C.4.b.description: Knowledge and prediction of physical principles, laws, their interrelationships, and applications to understanding fluid, material, and atmospheric dynamics, and mechanical, electrical, atomic and sub-atomic structures and processes.

2.C.4.c.name: Chemistry
2.C.4.c.description: Knowledge of the chemical composition, structure, and properties of substances and of the chemical processes and transformations that they undergo. This includes uses of chemicals and their interactions, danger signs, production techniques, and disposal methods.

2.C.4.d.name: Biology
2.C.4.d.description: Knowledge of plant and animal organisms, their tissues, cells, functions, interdependencies, and interactions with each other and the environment.

2.C.4.e.name: Psychology
2.C.4.e.description: Knowledge of human behavior and performance; individual differences in ability, personality, and interests; learning and motivation; psychological research methods; and the assessment and treatment of behavioral and affective disorders.

2.C.4.f.name: Sociology and Anthropology
2.C.4.f.description: Knowledge of group behavior and dynamics, societal trends and influences, human migrations, ethnicity, cultures, and their history and origins.

2.C.4.g.name: Geography
2.C.4.g.description: Knowledge of principles and methods for describing the features of land, sea, and air masses, including their physical characteristics, locations, interrelationships, and distribution of plant, animal, and human life.

2.C.5.name: Health Services
2.C.5.description: Knowledge of principles and facts regarding diagnosing, curing, and preventing disease, and improving and preserving physical and mental health and well-being

2.C.5.a.name: Medicine and Dentistry
2.C.5.a.description: Knowledge of the information and techniques needed to diagnose and treat human injuries, diseases, and deformities. This includes symptoms, treatment alternatives, drug properties and interactions, and preventive health-care measures.

2.C.5.b.name: Therapy and Counseling
2.C.5.b.description: Knowledge of principles, methods, and procedures for diagnosis, treatment, and rehabilitation of physical and mental dysfunctions, and for career counseling and guidance.

2.C.6.name: Education and Training
2.C.6.description: Knowledge of principles and methods for curriculum and training design, teaching and instruction for individuals and groups, and the measurement of training effects.

2.C.7.name: Arts and Humanities
2.C.7.description: Knowledge of facts and principles related to the branches of learning concerned with human thought, language, and the arts.

2.C.7.a.name: English Language
2.C.7.a.description: Knowledge of the structure and content of the English language including the meaning and spelling of words, and rules of composition and grammar.

2.C.7.b.name: Foreign Language
2.C.7.b.description: Knowledge of the structure and content of a foreign (non-English) language including the meaning and spelling of words, rules of composition and grammar, and pronunciation.

2.C.7.c.name: Fine Arts
2.C.7.c.description: Knowledge of the theory and techniques required to compose, produce, and perform works of music, dance, visual arts, drama, and sculpture.

2.C.7.d.name: History and Archeology
2.C.7.d.description: Knowledge of historical events and their causes, indicators, and effects on civilizations and cultures.

2.C.7.e.name: Philosophy and Theology
2.C.7.e.description: Knowledge of different philosophical systems and religions. This includes their basic principles, values, ethics, ways of thinking, customs, practices, and their impact on human culture.

2.C.8.name: Law and Public Safety
2.C.8.description: Knowledge of regulations and methods for maintaining people and property free from danger, injury, or damage; the rules of public conduct established and enforced by legislation, and the political process establishing such rules.

2.C.8.a.name: Public Safety and Security
2.C.8.a.description: Knowledge of relevant equipment, policies, procedures, and strategies to promote effective local, state, or national security operations for the protection of people, data, property, and institutions.

2.C.8.b.name: Law and Government
2.C.8.b.description: Knowledge of laws, legal codes, court procedures, precedents, government regulations, executive orders, agency rules, and the democratic political process.

2.C.9.name: Communications
2.C.9.description: Knowledge of the science and art of delivering information

2.C.9.a.name: Telecommunications
2.C.9.a.description: Knowledge of transmission, broadcasting, switching, control, and operation of telecommunications systems.

2.C.9.b.name: Communications and Media
2.C.9.b.description: Knowledge of media production, communication, and dissemination techniques and methods. This includes alternative ways to inform and entertain via written, oral, and visual media.

2.C.10.name: Transportation
2.C.10.description: Knowledge of principles and methods for moving people or goods by air, rail, sea, or road, including the relative costs and benefits.

2.C.1.a.name: Administration and Management
2.C.1.a.description: Knowledge of business and management principles involved in strategic planning, resource allocation, human resources modeling, leadership technique, production methods, and coordination of people and resources.

anchors.description: Approve a reimbursement request
Monitor progress of a project to ensure timely completion
Manage a multimillion-dollar company

2.C.1.b.name: Administrative
2.C.1.b.description: Knowledge of administrative and office procedures and systems such as word processing, managing files and records, stenography and transcription, designing forms, and workplace terminology.

anchors.description: Direct phone calls to the appropriate staff member
Type 30 words per minute
Organize a digital storage system for company forms

2.C.1.c.name: Economics and Accounting
2.C.1.c.description: Knowledge of economic and accounting principles and practices, the financial markets, banking, and the analysis and reporting of financial data.

anchors.description: Answer billing questions from credit card customers
Develop financial investment programs for individual clients
Keep a major corporation's financial records

2.C.1.d.name: Sales and Marketing
2.C.1.d.description: Knowledge of principles and methods for showing, promoting, and selling products or services. This includes marketing strategy and tactics, product demonstration, sales techniques, and sales control systems.

anchors.description: Sell cakes at a bake sale
Introduce clients to a new line of products
Develop a marketing plan for a new nationwide high-speed internet system

2.C.1.e.name: Customer and Personal Service
2.C.1.e.description: Knowledge of principles and processes for providing customer and personal services. This includes customer needs assessment, meeting quality standards for services, and evaluation of customer satisfaction.

anchors.description: Process a customer's dry-cleaning drop off
Be responsible for 10 children at daycare
Respond to a citizen's request for assistance after a major disaster

2.C.1.f.name: Personnel and Human Resources
2.C.1.f.description: Knowledge of principles and procedures for personnel recruitment, selection, training, compensation and benefits, labor relations and negotiation, and personnel information systems.

anchors.description: Fill out a medical claim form
Interview applicants for an administrative position
Design a new personnel selection and promotion system for the Army

2.C.10.name: Transportation
2.C.10.description: Knowledge of principles and methods for moving people or goods by air, rail, sea, or road, including the relative costs and benefits.

anchors.description: Ride a train to work
Drive a semi-truck through a busy city
Control air traffic at a busy airport

2.C.2.a.name: Production and Processing
2.C.2.a.description: Knowledge of raw materials, production processes, quality control, costs, and other techniques for maximizing the effective manufacture and distribution of goods.

anchors.description: Put a computer back into its packing materials
Supervise an appliance assembly line
Manage an international shipping company distribution center

2.C.2.b.name: Food Production
2.C.2.b.description: Knowledge of techniques and equipment for planting, growing, and harvesting food products (both plant and animal) for consumption, including storage/handling techniques.

anchors.description: Grow herbs in the kitchen
Operate a commercial fishing boat
Run a large production farm

2.C.3.a.name: Computers and Electronics
2.C.3.a.description: Knowledge of circuit boards, processors, chips, electronic equipment, and computer hardware and software, including applications and programming.

anchors.description: Operate a media player to watch a training video
Use a computer to format a document
Create a program to scan a computer for viruses

2.C.3.b.name: Engineering and Technology
2.C.3.b.description: Knowledge of the practical application of engineering science and technology. This includes applying principles, techniques, procedures, and equipment to the design and production of various goods and services.

anchors.description: Install a door lock
Design a custom office chair
Plan for the impact of weather in designing a bridge

2.C.3.c.name: Design
2.C.3.c.description: Knowledge of design techniques, tools, and principles involved in production of precision technical plans, blueprints, drawings, and models.

anchors.description: Draw a straight line 4 3/16 inches long
Design plans for remodeling a kitchen
Develop detailed plans for a high-rise office building

2.C.3.d.name: Building and Construction
2.C.3.d.description: Knowledge of materials, methods, and the tools involved in the construction or repair of houses, buildings, or other structures such as highways and roads.

anchors.description: Choose the proper type of wood for adding a deck onto a house
Fix a plumbing leak in the ceiling
Build a high-rise office tower

2.C.3.e.name: Mechanical
2.C.3.e.description: Knowledge of machines and tools, including their designs, uses, repair, and maintenance.

anchors.description: Replace the filters in a furnace
Replace a valve on a steam pipe
Overhaul an airplane jet engine

2.C.4.a.name: Mathematics
2.C.4.a.description: Knowledge of arithmetic, algebra, geometry, calculus, statistics, and their applications.

anchors.description: Add two numbers
Analyze data to determine areas with the highest sales
Derive a complex mathematical equation

2.C.4.b.name: Physics
2.C.4.b.description: Knowledge and prediction of physical principles, laws, their interrelationships, and applications to understanding fluid, material, and atmospheric dynamics, and mechanical, electrical, atomic and sub-atomic structures and processes.

anchors.description: Use a crowbar to pry open a box
Calculate the speed of a falling object
Design a cleaner burning gasoline engine

2.C.4.c.name: Chemistry
2.C.4.c.description: Knowledge of the chemical composition, structure, and properties of substances and of the chemical processes and transformations that they undergo. This includes uses of chemicals and their interactions, danger signs, production techniques, and disposal methods.

anchors.description: Use a common household bug spray
Use the proper concentration of chlorine to purify a water source
Develop a safe commercial cleaner

2.C.4.d.name: Biology
2.C.4.d.description: Knowledge of plant and animal organisms, their tissues, cells, functions, interdependencies, and interactions with each other and the environment.

anchors.description: Feed domestic animals
Investigate the effects of pollution on marine plants and animals
Isolate and identify a new virus

2.C.4.e.name: Psychology
2.C.4.e.description: Knowledge of human behavior and performance; individual differences in ability, personality, and interests; learning and motivation; psychological research methods; and the assessment and treatment of behavioral and affective disorders.

anchors.description: Monitor several children on a playground
Understand the impact of alcohol on human responses
Treat a person with a severe mental illness

2.C.4.f.name: Sociology and Anthropology
2.C.4.f.description: Knowledge of group behavior and dynamics, societal trends and influences, human migrations, ethnicity, cultures, and their history and origins.

anchors.description: Identify two cultures in a story as being different
Write an article about cultural differences
Create a new theory about the development of civilizations

2.C.4.g.name: Geography
2.C.4.g.description: Knowledge of principles and methods for describing the features of land, sea, and air masses, including their physical characteristics, locations, interrelationships, and distribution of plant, animal, and human life.

anchors.description: Know the capital of the United States
Find a specific country on a world map
Develop a map of the world showing mountains, deserts, and rivers

2.C.5.a.name: Medicine and Dentistry
2.C.5.a.description: Knowledge of the information and techniques needed to diagnose and treat human injuries, diseases, and deformities. This includes symptoms, treatment alternatives, drug properties and interactions, and preventive health-care measures.

anchors.description: Use a band-aid
Fill a tooth cavity
Perform open heart surgery

2.C.5.b.name: Therapy and Counseling
2.C.5.b.description: Knowledge of principles, methods, and procedures for diagnosis, treatment, and rehabilitation of physical and mental dysfunctions, and for career counseling and guidance.

anchors.description: Put ice on a sprained ankle
Provide job counseling to the unemployed
Counsel an abused child

2.C.6.name: Education and Training
2.C.6.description: Knowledge of principles and methods for curriculum and training design, teaching and instruction for individuals and groups, and the measurement of training effects.

anchors.description: Show someone how to bowl
Lead a quality improvement seminar
Design a training program for new employees

2.C.7.a.name: English Language
2.C.7.a.description: Knowledge of the structure and content of the English language including the meaning and spelling of words, and rules of composition and grammar.

anchors.description: Write a thank you note
Edit an article for a news website
Teach a college English class

2.C.7.b.name: Foreign Language
2.C.7.b.description: Knowledge of the structure and content of a foreign (non-English) language including the meaning and spelling of words, rules of composition and grammar, and pronunciation.

anchors.description: Say "please" and "thank you" in a foreign language
Ask directions in a foreign city
Write an English language review of a book written in a foreign language

2.C.7.c.name: Fine Arts
2.C.7.c.description: Knowledge of the theory and techniques required to compose, produce, and perform works of music, dance, visual arts, drama, and sculpture.

anchors.description: Attend a popular music concert
Play a minor part in a local theater play
Design an exhibit for a major art show

2.C.7.d.name: History and Archeology
2.C.7.d.description: Knowledge of historical events and their causes, indicators, and effects on civilizations and cultures.

anchors.description: Take a class in US history
Teach local history to school children
Determine the age of bones for placing them in fossil history

2.C.7.e.name: Philosophy and Theology
2.C.7.e.description: Knowledge of different philosophical systems and religions. This includes their basic principles, values, ethics, ways of thinking, customs, practices, and their impact on human culture.

anchors.description: Watch a TV show on family values
Understand another culture's religious practices
Compare the teachings of major philosophers

2.C.8.a.name: Public Safety and Security
2.C.8.a.description: Knowledge of relevant equipment, policies, procedures, and strategies to promote effective local, state, or national security operations for the protection of people, data, property, and institutions.

anchors.description: Use a seatbelt
Inspect a building site for safety violations
Command a military operation

2.C.8.b.name: Law and Government
2.C.8.b.description: Knowledge of laws, legal codes, court procedures, precedents, government regulations, executive orders, agency rules, and the democratic political process.

anchors.description: Describe the length of a U.S. presidential term
Prepare documents and title papers for the purchase of a new house
Serve as a judge in a federal court

2.C.9.a.name: Telecommunications
2.C.9.a.description: Knowledge of transmission, broadcasting, switching, control, and operation of telecommunications systems.

anchors.description: Send an email
Install a wireless internet router
Develop a new, world-wide telecommunications network

2.C.9.b.name: Communications and Media
2.C.9.b.description: Knowledge of media production, communication, and dissemination techniques and methods. This includes alternative ways to inform and entertain via written, oral, and visual media.

anchors.description: Write a thank you note
Host a music radio show
Write a novel
